lessonPurpose: Students learn the basic language, notation, and undefined terms used to describe geometric figures.
objectives: Identify points, lines, planes, rays, segments, and angles.
Recognize point, line, and plane as undefined terms.
Distinguish between collinear and coplanar points.

misconceptions.description: Students may confuse a ray with a segment.
misconceptions.recoveryFocus: Emphasize that a ray has one endpoint and extends forever in one direction.

misconceptions.description: Students may confuse a line with a segment.
misconceptions.recoveryFocus: Emphasize that a line extends forever in both directions, while a segment has two endpoints.

misconceptions.description: Students may think collinear and coplanar mean the same thing.
misconceptions.recoveryFocus: Collinear means on the same line. Coplanar means on the same plane.

misconceptions.description: Students may treat geometric notation as decoration instead of meaning.
misconceptions.recoveryFocus: Connect each notation format to the object it represents.

assessmentTargets.description: Identify point, line, and plane as undefined terms.
Identify basic geometric figures from descriptions.
Match geometric notation to its correct meaning.
Distinguish between collinear and coplanar relationships.
